snapshot.scope: Manual H3 review binds the Job, API, architecture, quality, playbook, decision and traceability owners to the exact 89d7ee9 coordinator candidate plus its current Planning bind and bounded Decision Log structure/leanness repair; automated gates cover implementation evidence separately.

reviewers.task: H3 JobExecutionCoordinator cancellation, resume proof, lifecycle ownership and renderer proposal containment review
reviewers.independence: The reviewer did not author the H3 code, Planning bind, or bounded Decision Log repair and compared the current documentation bytes and exact 89d7ee961d799c4710545b08980a4d8c71a874da implementation with H2 baseline b9b624df9ea13b17ac869e1193b88095f0ba3806 before issuing CLEAR.

dimensions.summary: Existing Job, API, architecture, quality, playbook and decision owners now expose one coherent H3 core without adding a competing document or changing the public product scope.

The documents match code: one coordinator owns migrated Jobs/Home lifecycle, cancellation has three proof-dependent terminal projections, resume proofs are exact, and raw proposal renderer access remains fail closed.

Three exact H3 evidence selectors bind lifecycle, cancellation and resume-proof claims while PIGE-JOB-002, PIGE-JOB-004 and E2.10/E2.11 remain partial and every Requirement, Exit and Phase status is unchanged.

The migration is executable and reviewable: JobsService has zero direct lifecycle-state writers, two named CAS compatibility boundaries remain, Home adopts durable completion, and H3b/H3c stay explicit.
No new owner document was added. Existing Job and decision owners carry the H3 contract; trace and review locks remain machine-bound.

verification.evidence: Exact focused review passed coordinator, Jobs, Home retry, permission and model-egress recovery coverage, including all cancellation outcomes and exact resume proofs.
The responsibility gate reports zero direct JobsService lifecycle-state writers, exactly two named CAS compatibility boundaries and explicit H3b/H3c residual loops.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, parser manifests, PRD contract and Decision Log checks pass on the current H3 governed snapshot.

verification.command: Independent code, decision and traceability review of current H3 documentation bytes and 89d7ee961d799c4710545b08980a4d8c71a874da against b9b624df9ea13b17ac869e1193b88095f0ba3806
verification.evidence: Exact hashes and code review confirm three cancellation projections, exact repair/source proofs, raw proposal removal, unchanged Development bytes and no Requirement, Exit or Phase promotion.

coordination.taskTitle: H3 JobExecutionCoordinator core and Jobs/Home migration
coordination.compatibility: H3 centralizes migrated Jobs/Home lifecycle without changing Pi semantic ownership, durable schema, renderer authority or non-migrated Job-class behavior.
coordination.acknowledgement: Planning synchronized only existing H3 owner and traceability resources after independent CLEAR; Development/UI bytes remain unchanged, H3b/H3c stay serialized open work, and status projections remain unchanged.

nonBlockingRisks: Backup, Restore and Local Tool lifecycle loops remain outside the coordinator core and must move in H3b without creating a second transition owner.
JobsService still owns dispatch; H3c must introduce a per-class executor registry while preserving exact Job and durable-effect proofs.
The preload type surface still names legacy proposal methods, but Main rejects them and renderer no longer calls or renders the raw DTO; a bounded preview and decision owner remains open.
Coordinator, JobsService and Home remain large; the current ownership change is not evidence of global service convergence or completion.
Cross-file atomicity, final-syscall TOCTOU, Pi transcript continuation and remaining Job-class recovery are open.
Windows/Linux qualification remains deferred and unclaimed; Requirement, Exit and Phase statuses are unchanged.
